steps.observed: Report page loaded successfully with all sections
Screenshot Timeline heading (h2) present with description 'Chronological evidence captured during the audit journey'

steps.expected: Screenshots with captions showing scenario, persona, and step context
steps.observed: 6 timeline entries found: Step 1-6, each with persona label (Privacy Sensitive, Cost Sensitive, Exit Intent), step context (Live 1, Live 2), scenario (Checkout Flow), and timestamp (Mar 12, 2026, 6:41 AM). Each entry includes a clickable screenshot thumbnail.

evidence.network: Page loaded successfully, screenshot images loaded from backend API

steps.action: Scroll to Findings and evidence section
steps.expected: Findings grouped by scenario with headings
steps.observed: Findings and evidence heading (h2) with description 'Grouped by scenario and persona so stakeholder teams can triage remediations quickly'
'Checkout Flow' heading (h3) present as the scenario group heading

Three persona sub-groups found under Checkout Flow: 'Privacy Sensitive', 'Cost Sensitive', 'Exit Intent'. Each sub-group contains a finding card with severity badge, obstruction category, confidence percentage, and detailed evidence.

assertions.title: PDF export

steps.action: Locate Download PDF button on report page
steps.expected: Download PDF button visible and clickable
steps.observed: Download PDF button present, styled as a green pill button, links to http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/audits/adcde59c-d03d-41c5-be90-9fdf4d3e96f8/report/pdf

steps.expected: PDF file downloads with valid content containing trust score, risk level, and findings
steps.observed: Backend returns HTTP 500 error: 'PDF generation failed: Invalid color value <css function: var(--line)>'. The HTML report endpoint (/api/audits/{id}/report) works fine (returns 200 with valid HTML), but the PDF conversion fails due to a CSS variable resolution error in the backend PDF generator.

assertions.issues: PDF generation fails with HTTP 500: 'PDF generation failed: Invalid color value <css function: var(--line)>'. The CSS custom property var(--line) is not being resolved before being passed to the PDF renderer. The Download PDF button UI element exists and links correctly, but the backend PDF generation pipeline has a CSS variable resolution bug.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 2 passed (VAL-UI-007 screenshot timeline, VAL-UI-008 findings grouped by scenario), 1 failed (VAL-UI-009 PDF export - backend PDF generation returns 500 due to unresolved CSS variable).
